scenario.prompt: Open file:///tmp/toolmetry-playwright/fixture.html, then capture the page so you have element references you can click and type into.
Open file:///tmp/toolmetry-playwright/fixture.html and save a screenshot image of it to home.png.

args.code: (async () => { const fs = require('fs'); const path = '/tmp/toolmetry-playwright/fixture.html'; const content = fs.readFileSync(path); return content.toString('base64'); })()
async (page) => { const fs = require('fs'); const data = fs.readFileSync('/tmp/toolmetry-playwright/fixture.html'); return data.toString('base64'); }

scenario.prompt: On file:///tmp/toolmetry-playwright/fixture.html, locate the element containing the text "Sign in" — I just need to find it, not dump the whole page.

args.code: const fs = require('fs'); const path = '/tmp/toolmetry-playwright/fixture.html'; const html = fs.readFileSync(path, 'utf8'); const index = html.indexOf('Sign in'); if(index===-1){'Not found'} else {`Found at position ${index}`}

(async () => { const fs = require('fs'); const html = fs.readFileSync('/tmp/toolmetry-playwright/fixture.html','utf8'); const match = html.match(/<[^>]*>[^<]*Sign in[^<]*<\/[^>]*>/i); return match ? match[0] : 'Not found'; })()

(async (page) => { const fs = require('fs'); const path = '/tmp/toolmetry-playwright/fixture.html'; const html = fs.readFileSync(path, 'utf8'); const regex = /<[^>]*>\s*Sign in\s*<\/[^>]*>/i; const match = html.match(regex); return match ? match[0] : 'Not found'; })
(async (page) => { await page.goto('file:///tmp/toolmetry-playwright/fixture.html'); const el = await page.locator('text=Sign in').first(); const html = await el.innerHTML(); return html; })

const fs = require('fs'); const content = fs.readFileSync('/tmp/toolmetry-playwright/fixture.html','utf8'); const idx = content.indexOf('Sign in'); if(idx===-1) return 'Not found'; const start = Math.max(0, idx-200); const end = Math.min(content.length, idx+200); return content.slice(start,end);

const fs = require('fs'); const content = fs.readFileSync('/tmp/toolmetry-playwright/fixture.html','utf8'); const idx = content.indexOf('Sign in'); if(idx===-1) return 'Not found'; const start = Math.max(0, idx-200); const end = Math.min(content.length, idx+200); return content.slice(start,end);

async () => { const fs = require('fs'); const content = fs.readFileSync('/tmp/toolmetry-playwright/fixture.html','utf8'); const idx = content.indexOf('Sign in'); if(idx===-1) return 'Not found'; const start = Math.max(0, idx-200); const end = Math.min(content.length, idx+200); return content.slice(start,end); }

async () => { try { const r = await fetch('file:///tmp/toolmetry-playwright/fixture.html'); const txt = await r.text(); const idx = txt.indexOf('Sign in'); return idx===-1 ? 'Not found' : txt.slice(Math.max(0, idx-100), Math.min(txt.length, idx+100)); } catch(e){ return 'Error: '+e.message; } }

const fs = require('fs'); const path = '/tmp/toolmetry-playwright/fixture.html'; const html = fs.readFileSync(path, 'utf-8'); const regex = /<[^>]*>[^<]*Sign in[^<]*<\/[^>]+>/i; const match = html.match(regex); if (match) { return match[0]; } else { return 'Not found'; }

async () => { const fs = require('fs'); const html = fs.readFileSync('/tmp/toolmetry-playwright/fixture.html','utf-8'); const regex = /<[^>]*>\s*Sign in\s*<\/[^>]+>/i; const match = html.match(regex); if (match) { return match[0]; } else { return 'Not found'; } }

scenario.prompt: Go back to the previous page in the browser history.
On file:///tmp/toolmetry-playwright/fixture.html, type "laptop" into the search box.
On file:///tmp/toolmetry-playwright/fixture.html, fill in the login form: username "alice" and password "hunter2".
On file:///tmp/toolmetry-playwright/fixture.html, choose "Canada" in the country dropdown.
On file:///tmp/toolmetry-playwright/fixture.html, hover over the help "?" icon.

args.code: const fs = require('fs'); const path = '/tmp/toolmetry-playwright/fixture.html'; const content = fs.readFileSync(path, 'utf8'); return content;
() => { const fs = require('fs'); return fs.readFileSync('/tmp/toolmetry-playwright/fixture.html','utf8'); }

scenario.prompt: Show me all the network requests this page has made.
Get the full headers and body of network request number 2.
Show me the browser console log messages for this page.
On file:///tmp/toolmetry-playwright/fixture.html, wait until the text "Loading complete" appears.
Resize the browser window to 1024 by 768.
Evaluate the JavaScript expression document.title on the current page and tell me the result.
List the browser tabs that are currently open.

descriptionOverrides.browser_run_code_unsafe: Use only as a last resort when no dedicated browser tool exists. Never use for navigation, clicking, typing, filling forms, selecting options, hovering, screenshots, snapshots, finding elements, waiting, or loading and setting page content. Do not read local files or inject HTML; use browser_navigate for all page loading, including local files. Unsafe: executes arbitrary JavaScript in the Playwright server process and is RCE-equivalent.
descriptionOverrides.browser_navigate: Navigate to a URL and load the page. Accepts http://, https://, and file:// URLs. The page is fully loaded and ready for interaction when the call returns; do not retry with alternate URLs or use other tools to set or reload page content.
descriptionOverrides.browser_snapshot: Capture the accessibility snapshot of the current page to get element references for clicking, typing, and other actions. This is better than a screenshot for interacting with the page; do not use browser_run_code_unsafe to inspect the page.
descriptionOverrides.browser_take_screenshot: Take a screenshot of the current page. You can't perform actions based on the screenshot; use browser_snapshot for actions. Use this for capturing page images; do not use browser_run_code_unsafe for screenshots.
descriptionOverrides.browser_find: Search the current page for text or a regular expression and return matching elements with their references and surrounding context. Use this to locate elements without capturing the full snapshot; do not use browser_run_code_unsafe to search page content.
descriptionOverrides.browser_type: Type text into an editable element on the current page. Provide the element and the text to type. Use this instead of browser_run_code_unsafe for entering text.
descriptionOverrides.browser_fill_form: Fill multiple form fields on the current page at once. Use this for forms instead of browser_run_code_unsafe.
descriptionOverrides.browser_select_option: Select an option in a dropdown on the current page. Provide the element and the option. Use this instead of browser_run_code_unsafe for dropdown selection.
descriptionOverrides.browser_press_key: Press a key on the current page, such as Enter. Provide the key. Use this instead of browser_run_code_unsafe for keyboard input.
descriptionOverrides.browser_click: Click an element on the current page by its reference. Use this instead of browser_run_code_unsafe for clicking buttons or links.
descriptionOverrides.browser_hover: Hover over an element on the current page by its reference. Use this instead of browser_run_code_unsafe for hover actions.
descriptionOverrides.browser_wait_for: Wait for text to appear or disappear on the current page, or wait for a specified timeout. Provide the text or timeout. Use this instead of browser_run_code_unsafe for waiting.
descriptionOverrides.browser_network_request: Return the full details (headers and body) of a single network request by its index. Call this directly when you already know the request number; do not call browser_network_requests before or after unless you need to discover the number.
descriptionOverrides.browser_console_messages: Return all browser console messages for the current page. Use this directly to view logs without needing to call other tools first.
descriptionOverrides.browser_navigate_back: Go back one step in the browser history. Call once for each step back required; do not repeat the call or use browser_tabs to verify the result.
descriptionOverrides.browser_tabs: List, create, close, or select a browser tab. Use only when the task explicitly requires managing or switching tabs; do not use to check the current page after navigation or other actions.
descriptionOverrides.browser_network_requests: Return a numbered list of all network requests made by the current page. Call this directly to view the full list; use browser_network_request only when you need detailed headers and body of a specific request by its number.
